risk_if_not_implemented: Without Regional Delivery, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Network Security Controls (NSC)
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to develop, govern & update procedures to facilitate the implementation of Network Security Controls (NSC).
compensating_control_1.justification: Network Security Controls (NSC) (NET-01) provides network-level access restriction that compensates for the absence of Regional Delivery (CAP-06) by limiting attacker reach and lateral movement opportunities across the environment. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Telecommunications Services Availability
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to reduce the likelihood of a single point of failure with primary telecommunications services.
compensating_control_2.justification: Telecommunications Services Availability (BCD-10) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Regional Delivery (CAP-06) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
